title: Why Money Matters
body:
Money matters because it changes the set of possible actions available to a person. A small buffer can prevent a crisis from becoming a disaster.

It also matters because bad money habits compound. Fees, consumer debt, and poor planning can quietly drain years of effort.

Good money habits create optionality. They give people room to choose better work, better housing, and better timing instead of reacting in panic.
keyPoints: A small emergency buffer can prevent major stress
Fees and debt shape outcomes more than many people realise
Money literacy improves choice, not just wealth
Financial pressure leaks into health and relationships
Simplicity often beats complicated money systems
furtherReading: The Psychology of Money by Morgan Housel
Money: Master the Game by Tony Robbins
